{"id":"project-LICHEN-83zg","title":"visualize.to_dot doesn't escape special characters in node labels","description":"File: python/src/lichen/rpl/visualize.py:66-78

The to_dot() function generates Graphviz DOT output but doesn't escape special characters in node labels:

```python
for node in sorted(parents):
    label = node
    if ranks is not None and node in ranks:
        label = f"{node}\\nrank={ranks[node]}"
    attrs = f'label="{label}"'  # No escaping of node content
```

If a node ID contains characters special to DOT syntax (quotes, backslashes, braces), the generated DOT would be malformed:
- Node "fe80::1" works fine
- Node 'test"node' would break: label="test"node"
- Node 'a\b' would break: label="a\b" (backslash interpreted)

IPv6 addresses don't typically contain these characters, but the function accepts arbitrary strings per its type signature.

Fix: Escape the label:
```python
def escape_dot(s: str) -> str:
    return s.replace('\\', '\\\\').replace('"', '\\"')

label = escape_dot(node)
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:54.8858Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:54.885838Z"}

new_value: Added _escape_dot helper and updated to_dot() to escape " and \ in node IDs and labels (python/src/lichen/rpl/visualize.py:69). Existing tests + manual cases with quotes/backslash pass; ruff clean. Completed 3 independent codereview passes (lint+test each time).
